subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Bone Healing
description: A fast breakdown of Bone Healing for builders.

hook.text: A broken bone heals stronger at the break point than the original. Your body over-engineers repairs.

buildup.text: When a bone breaks, a blood clot forms between the fragments called a fracture hematoma.

discovery.text: The body builds a soft callus of cartilage, then slowly replaces it with hard bone.

twist.text: Bone healing requires alignment—misaligned bones heal in the wrong position permanently.

climax.text: That's why splinting and immobilization are critical first aid for suspected fractures.

punchline.text: Hold it still. The bone will rebuild itself. Just keep it straight.

quiz.question: Why is immobilization critical for bone fractures?
options: Misaligned bones heal in the wrong position
